content: If we map the human language learning process of repeated reflective vocabulary and writing reinforcement (originating from the lexical definition of reflexion in high school English curricula) onto LLM iterative self-improvement frameworks, we can build a linguistically-grounded warm-up stage for new domain-specific LLMs that mimics how human students internalize knowledge through structured reflection. This creates a low-compute alternative to traditional large-scale pre-training for domain specialization that aligns LLM output more closely with natural human rhetorical and conceptual patterns.
